channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. strikes Iran in response to a drone attack on a ship
item.description: The U.S. struck Iran on Friday in response to a drone attack a day earlier on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz. It's the most significant test yet to an interim understanding reached a week ago by the two countries.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. struck Iran on Friday in response to a drone attack a day earlier on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz. It's the most significant test yet to an interim understanding reached a week ago by the two countries.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. pledges generous earthquake relief to Venezuela
item.description: Following the earthquakes in Venezuela, the Trump administration announced what appears to be its strongest disaster response since it dismantled America's premier aid agency USAID last year.
item.content:encoded: Following the earthquakes in Venezuela, the Trump administration announced what appears to be its strongest disaster response since it dismantled America's premier aid agency USAID last year.
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis

item.title: It's not just Cape Verde: African soccer is having a World Cup moment
item.description: The soccer greats — Brazil, England, Spain — have been giving uneven performances out on the pitch during this World Cup. Meanwhile, teams from Africa have been relentless.
item.content:encoded:
The soccer greats — Brazil, England, Spain — have been giving uneven performances out on the pitch during this World Cup. Meanwhile, teams from Africa have been relentless.

(Image credit: Carolina Herrera for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Garsd

item.title: Palestinians grieve for a father-to-be shot by Israeli troops the day his son was born
item.description: Nayef Samaro, 25, was killed in an Israeli military raid on Nablus, in the occupied West Bank, as he prepared to meet his wife at the hospital for the birth of their first child.
item.content:encoded:
Nayef Samaro, 25, was killed in an Israeli military raid on Nablus, in the occupied West Bank, as he prepared to meet his wife at the hospital for the birth of their first child.

(Image credit: Ruth Sherlock)
item.dc:creator: Ruth Sherlock

item.title: Ukraine unleashes one of its heaviest drone bombardments, as Russia strikes Ukraine
item.description: Ukraine launched a major nighttime attack on a dozen Russian regions, Russian-held Crimea and the surrounding seas, and Russia attacked various regions of Ukraine, as their war grinds on.
item.content:encoded:
Ukraine launched a major nighttime attack on a dozen Russian regions, Russian-held Crimea and the surrounding seas, and Russia attacked various regions of Ukraine, as their war grinds on.

(Image credit: Kateryna Klochko)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Plane crashes into Beijing's tallest building; damage reported
item.description: The cause of the damage could not be independently verified, and authorities did not immediately issue a statement on the incident.
item.content:encoded:
The cause of the damage could not be independently verified, and authorities did not immediately issue a statement on the incident.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Australia plans to strengthen laws banning children from social media
item.description: Observers said on Friday the government is responding to evidence that the ban on young children holding accounts on platforms including Facebook, Instagram and YouTube had failed since it came into force in December year.
item.content:encoded:
Observers said on Friday the government is responding to evidence that the ban on young children holding accounts on platforms including Facebook, Instagram and YouTube had failed since it came into force in December year.

(Image credit: Rick Rycroft)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: South Korean ex-first lady sentenced to 7 years for bribery scandal
item.description: The former first lady had previously been sentenced to four years in a separate case involving the Unification Church.
item.content:encoded:
The former first lady had previously been sentenced to four years in a separate case involving the Unification Church.

(Image credit: Jung Yeon-je)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Rescuers scramble to find Venezuela earthquake survivors. And, SCOTUS rules on asylum
item.description: Rescuers are searching for thousands of people missing after two earthquakes struck Venezuela on Wednesday. And, the Supreme Court says the U.S. can turn away asylum seekers at the border.
item.content:encoded:
Rescuers are searching for thousands of people missing after two earthquakes struck Venezuela on Wednesday. And, the Supreme Court says the U.S. can turn away asylum seekers at the border.

(Image credit: Jesus Vargas)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Aid worker says hundreds remain trapped after Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: After earthquakes shattered much of the capital in Venezuela, Project Hope's Cesar Jimenez says rescue crews are racing to save hundreds trapped as international aid arrives and hospitals struggle to cope.
item.content:encoded:
After earthquakes shattered much of the capital in Venezuela, Project Hope's Cesar Jimenez says rescue crews are racing to save hundreds trapped as international aid arrives and hospitals struggle to cope.

(Image credit: Javier Campos)
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel
